hero.title_html: Industrial supervision without tag pricing, with your data in Power BI.
hero.subtitle: Wiregrid reads your Modbus TCP, OPC-UA and BACnet PLCs, writes every measurement into a standard PostgreSQL database, and makes it directly queryable from Power BI, Grafana or Excel — no export, no middleware, no per-tag licensing.
hero.cta_note: Public demo, no sign-up. Auth and data residency available when self-hosted.

app_preview.aria_label: Wiregrid interface preview: live points table
app_preview.title: Live points

comparison.title: Why not Niagara or Ignition?
comparison.subtitle: Wiregrid does not try to replace Niagara N4 on a 5,000-point site with a full wiresheet and mature HMI builder. It targets the cases where you mostly pay for tags, on features that PostgreSQL + Power BI already do better.
comparison.disclaimer: Public information observed in April 2026. Niagara, Ignition and EcoStruxure are trademarks of their respective owners (Tridium, Inductive Automation, Schneider Electric). Factual comparison, not contractual.

how.title: From install to first dashboard, in three steps
how.subtitle: No mandatory integration services. You can do it yourself on a Sunday evening, or ask us for a hand.

1_install.title: Install
1_install.desc: Single Docker command on a VPS, an industrial mini-PC or a Raspberry Pi 4. The image bundles gateway-py, PostgreSQL 16 + TimescaleDB, Redis and the UI. No JVM, no license to activate.

2_configure.title: Connect
2_configure.desc: Declare your PLCs by IP and port. Wiregrid lists the exposed points, you organise them into machines and tags (site, building, function…), wire threshold alarms with deadband and delay. Every change is versioned — a bad deploy reverts in one click.

3_operate.title: Operate
3_operate.desc: Values stream over WebSocket into the UI (1–2 ms latency). In parallel, your PostgreSQL database is open for direct read from Power BI, Grafana, Metabase or Excel — your BI teams build dashboards without waiting for industrial IT.

features.title: Capabilities shipping today
features.subtitle: What the public demo actually does, not a roadmap. The wiresheet (visual programmable logic, Niagara-style) is in progress and lands in Q3 2026.

drivers.title: Multi-protocol acquisition
drivers.desc: Modbus TCP, OPC-UA and BACnet/IP in a single install. Each driver runs in its own asyncio task with independent polling per device and automatic reconnection. The demo bundles 10 drivers across the three protocols.

live.title: Live points over WebSocket
live.desc: Direct push from Redis to the browser via /ws/live. Typical latency 1–2 ms. Falls back to 1 s polling with exponential reconnect when the WS drops. Read and write supported on commandable points.

historian.title: TimescaleDB historian
historian.desc: All values are written to a TimescaleDB hypertable chunked by day. Per-point on_change with deadband or fixed-interval mode. Configurable retention, queryable in standard SQL or via time_bucket() for downsampling.

alarms.title: Operational alarms
alarms.desc: High/low conditions with threshold, deadband and delay. Severities info / warning / critical, state machine normal → active → acknowledged → cleared. Email notifications via user groups. Full event log for ISO traceability.

revisions.title: Git-style versioning
revisions.desc: Every config change (driver, point, machine, alarm, tag…) goes into a revision. Diff between two revisions, single-transaction revert to a past state, runtime hot-reload via pg_notify. You test on the working copy, you deploy when it's ready.

integrations.title: Open API + direct SQL access
integrations.desc: REST on /py/v1/{drivers,machines,points,alarms,revisions}, /api/v1 alias for legacy integrations. WebSocket /ws/live for push. And most importantly: Postgres open on the internal network — Power BI connects via ODBC without going through us.

multitenant.title: Multi-project / multi-site
multitenant.desc: Project_id scoping on every entity. Ideal to manage several buildings or several customers from a single instance. The demo signup flow clones a complete project (10 drivers / 13 machines / 18 alarms) in under a second.

wiresheet.title: Visual programmable logic
wiresheet.desc: Niagara N4-style functional block editor: math, comparison, timer, schedule, latch, ramp, PID. The execution engine is already in the runtime — the Vue Flow editor lands in Q3 2026.

equipment.title: PLC and instrument compatibility
equipment.subtitle: Wiregrid speaks 3 open protocols, which covers nearly every industrial PLC out there. The brands below have been tested or are compatible via the standard protocol spec.
equipment.note: Any device speaking Modbus TCP, OPC-UA or BACnet/IP is compatible — the list above is representative, not exhaustive. For a specific PLC with old firmware, plan a half-day validation session. Modbus RTU over RS-485 needs a TCP gateway (Moxa, USR-TCP232 or equivalent).

pricing.title: Simple, surprise-free pricing
pricing.subtitle: Free tier up to 50 points to evaluate or cover a small site. Beyond that, a flat annual fee — no per-tag licensing, no hidden runtime cost.
pricing.footnote: Prices excl. VAT, billed annually. 12-month commitment. You stay the owner of your data and your instance — Wiregrid has no access to your PostgreSQL.

faq.title: Questions we get
faq.subtitle: If yours isn't here, drop us a line at contact@wiregrid.fr — we reply within one business day.

difference_niagara.q: What's the actual difference vs Niagara N4?
difference_niagara.a_html: Niagara is more mature, with a huge feature set — full wiresheet, HMI builder, JACE ecosystem. Wiregrid is smaller, more modern (pure web, no JVM station), and most importantly without tag pricing: the ~€5/tag/year you pay on Niagara, you don't pay with us. Wiregrid fits when you have 50–500 points, not 5,000, and you want your data in Power BI.

compatible_plc.q: Compatible with my Schneider M340 / Siemens S7-1500 / other PLC?
compatible_plc.a_html: If your PLC speaks Modbus TCP (most Schneider, ABB, Wago, Phoenix Contact), OPC-UA (Siemens S7-1500 natively, Beckhoff TwinCAT, B&R) or BACnet/IP (Distech, Honeywell, Trend, JCI), yes. For Modbus RTU over serial, plan a TCP gateway like Moxa. See the Equipment section.

data_format.q: What format is the data stored in?
data_format.a_html: Configuration in standard PostgreSQL tables (drivers, points, machines, alarms…). Value history in a TimescaleDB hypertable point_history(ts, point_id, value, quality), chunked by day. Everything is queryable in plain SQL, no proprietary dialect.

powerbi.q: How do I connect Power BI?
powerbi.a_html: Power BI Desktop > Get Data > PostgreSQL. You enter your Wiregrid server's IP (port 5432), a read-only user, and you see the tables. No proprietary connector to install, no extra license to buy.

self_host.a_html: Self-hosted. docker compose up -d on any server — your data stays with you. The online demo runs on a public environment but it's a demonstration, not a production offer. A managed SaaS option will follow demand.

minimum_hardware.a_html: 2 vCPU, 4 GB RAM, 20 GB disk are enough for ~500 points with 30 days of history. Runs on an industrial mini-PC, a Raspberry Pi 4 or a €5/month Hetzner VPS. The demo currently runs on a €4/month nano.

security.a_html: JWT auth (UI) or API key (integrations). Postgres and Redis bind to localhost, never exposed on the internet. If you put the gateway behind a reverse proxy (Caddy, Nginx, Cloudflare Tunnel), you get automatic TLS. Zero outbound calls to Wiregrid in self-hosted mode.

safety_sil.q: Can I run functional safety (SIL) on it?
safety_sil.a_html: No, and never will. Wiregrid is a supervision and historian platform, not a critical control system. Your safety interlocks must stay in your certified PLC. Wiregrid reads, alarms, archives — it doesn't decide on your behalf.

open_source.a_html: Not publicly yet. The license will be decided at public release (candidate: AGPL-3.0 or source-available). In the meantime: all your data is in your PostgreSQL in standard SQL, so even if Wiregrid disappeared tomorrow, you keep your data and your history usable with any other tool.

company_disappears.q: What if you shut down tomorrow?
company_disappears.a_html: Your data lives in a standard PostgreSQL on your server. Your Power BI dashboards keep working. For acquisition, you replace Wiregrid with any other platform speaking Modbus / OPC-UA / BACnet — the equipment-wiring work is done once, not for the lifetime of the vendor.

multi_site.a_html: Today: multi-project on a single instance (great for multi-building setups within one group). For multi-site with edge gateways at each location plus a consolidating central, that's on the Q4 2026 roadmap.

wiresheet_status.q: When does the wiresheet (visual logic) ship?
wiresheet_status.a_html: The execution engine is already in the runtime. The Vue Flow editor (block palette, drag-drop, links) is targeted for Q3 2026. In the meantime, you can write complex alarm rules and trigger actions through email notifications.

support.a_html: Discovery plan: online documentation and public GitHub issues. Production plan: email with a 1-business-day response SLA, video calls on request, direct access to the lead developer.

trial.q: How long do I need to seriously try it?
trial.a_html: The online demo works as an instant POC (60 seconds of clicking around). For a trial on your own equipment, plan 1–2 hours to wire up a PLC and see your real points. Past that, the Discovery plan (50 points free) already covers a complete small site.

footer.tagline: Open web SCADA. Your Modbus / OPC-UA / BACnet PLCs, your data in standard Postgres, directly queryable from Power BI.
